title: Marriages and Divorces - Our World in Data
Marriages in the US are back to pre-pandemic levels, CDC says
snippet: The number and rate of U.S. divorces in 2022 fell slightly, continuing a downward trend, the CDC said. Overall, marriages remain far less common than they once were in the U.S.

snippet: Divorce rate: 2.4 per 1,000 population (45 reporting States and D.C.) Sources: National Marriage and Divorce Rate Trends for 2000-2023 [PDF – 117 KB] (data shown are provisional 2023)

snippet: Today, the national marriage rate is on the decline. During the past few years, the percentage of people getting married in the United States has been steadily dropping. The last time the marriage rate …

title: Marriage rates are up in the US and reaching a pre-pandemic high: CDC
snippet: Mar 18, 2024 · In the first two years following the arrival of COVID in the U.S., marriages bumped up to a rate of 6.2 per 1,000 people in 2022 from just 5.1 in 2020, the lowest in 20 years.

title: Marriages in the U.S. are back to pre-pandemic levels, according ... - PBS

title: Marriage and Divorce - Census.gov
snippet: Jun 17, 2025 · The U.S. Census Bureau provides the current marital status of persons, by age, sex, and race and the estimated median age at first marriage for men and women at the U.S. level.

title: The U.S. Marriage Rate Rebounds to Its Pre-Pandemic Level
snippet: Sep 15, 2023 · The rise of the marriage rate in 2022 reflects a natural rebound. The U.S. marriage rate was at its lowest level in 2020, when many couples had to delay weddings because of the pandemic.
